questionHtml: Explain why there is a large increase from the 8th to the 9th ionization energy of iron.
markschemeHtml:
Any two of:

IE9: electron in lower energy level
OR
IE9: more stable/full electron level ✔


IE9: electron closer to nucleus
OR
IE9: electron more tightly held by nucleus ✔


IE9: less shielding by «complete» inner levels ✔

meta.title: Explain why there is a large increase from the 8th to the 9th ionization energy of iron.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 1. Models of the particulate nature of matter
Structure 1.3.7—Successive ionization energy (IE) data for an element give information about its electron configuration. Deduce the group of an element from its successive ionization data.
